world.title: Did Pete Hegseth quote Pulp Fiction verse at prayer meet in Pentagon? Here is the truth
world.summary: Pete Hegseth reportedly quoted a line from Quentin Tarantino's film Pulp Fiction during a prayer meeting at the Pentagon. The film is recognized as a cultural icon, allowing its quotes to be referenced without context. The incident sparked discussions about the appropriateness of using movie lines in serious settings, highlighting the film's lasting impact on popular culture.

world.title: India faces energy squeeze as US ends oil waivers
world.summary: The U.S. has allowed Iranian and Russian oil waivers to expire, potentially tightening India's crude oil supply. India previously relied on these waivers for temporary relief in sustaining its oil imports. This decision may impact India's energy security and increase reliance on alternative sources, as the country navigates the challenges posed by reduced access to these oil supplies.

world.title: Express Entry pool grows larger, more competitive
world.summary: Canada’s Express Entry pool increased by over 2,100 profiles from March 15 to April 12, ending a months-long decline. This growth occurred despite the immigration department issuing nearly 14,000 Invitations to Apply (ITAs) during the same timeframe. The majority of new profiles were in the 451–480 Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score range, with significant additions also in the 501–600 range.

business.title: 'Dire strait': Europe may run out of jet fuel in 6 weeks warns IEA chief amid Iran war crisis
business.summary: The head of the International Energy Agency warned that Europe may have only six weeks of jet fuel remaining. This shortage could lead to flight cancellations if oil supplies are disrupted further due to the ongoing crisis related to the Iran war. The situation highlights the potential impact on air travel and energy security in the region.

business.title: HDFC Life Q4 Results: HDFC Bank to increase stake in the company as profit rises 4% YoY
business.summary: HDFC Life Insurance will issue shares worth one thousand crore rupees to HDFC Bank to enhance solvency and support growth. The company reported a 4% year-on-year profit increase for the March quarter, alongside a rise in net premium income. Additionally, the board has recommended a final dividend for FY26, reflecting the company's positive financial performance.

business.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
business.summary: Cisco has released patches for four critical vulnerabilities in its Identity Services Engine (ISE) and Webex, rated up to CVSS 9.9. These flaws could allow arbitrary code execution and enable user impersonation. The updates aim to mitigate risks associated with these security issues, ensuring enhanced protection for users of both services against potential attacks.

technology.title: Turtle Beach's Next Stealth Pro Headset Sounds Bigger and Badder
technology.summary: Turtle Beach has announced the Stealth Pro 2, a sequel to its popular Stealth Pro wireless headset. The new model features a redesigned look reminiscent of Apple's AirPods Max and includes larger 60mm drivers for enhanced audio quality. These multiplatform over-ear headphones aim to improve performance and comfort, catering to gamers and audio enthusiasts alike.

technology.title: Faster, Real-Time: Enhanced ‘Audio Eraser’ With the Galaxy S26 Series
technology.summary: The Galaxy S26 series features an enhanced Audio Eraser that allows real-time sound separation and optimization of voices, music, and background noise. This technology improves the listening experience by reducing unwanted noise in videos while amplifying desired sounds. Originally introduced with the Galaxy S25 series for editing, the feature has undergone significant advancements for better performance.

technology.title: Project Apple 2030 gathers pace as recycled materials hit record 30 per cent across 2025 products
technology.summary: Apple's Project 2030 is advancing as the company reports that 30% of materials used in its 2025 products will be recycled. This initiative aims to enhance sustainability within the tech industry. The MacBook Neo is highlighted as a significant product in this effort, contributing to Apple's commitment to reducing environmental impact and promoting the use of recycled materials.

sports.title: Krunal Pandya Puts To Rest All Social Media Buzz About Brother Hardik Pandya With One Reply
sports.summary: Krunal Pandya addressed social media speculation regarding his brother Hardik Pandya, confirming their status as the only star brother duo in IPL 2026. Krunal plays for the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, while Hardik leads the Mumbai Indians, who have won the championship five times. Their participation has generated significant interest ahead of the upcoming IPL season.

sports.title: Ankle injury rules David Payne out of IPL 2026
sports.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad's David Payne has been ruled out of IPL 2026 due to an ankle injury, increasing the team's list of unavailable players. Payne was initially brought in as a replacement for Jack Edwards. In contrast, team captain Pat Cummins has been cleared to rejoin the squad after undergoing scans on his back in Sydney.

sports.title: Ankle injury rules David Payne out of IPL 2026
sports.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad's David Payne has been ruled out of IPL 2026 due to an ankle injury, increasing the team's list of absentees. Payne was initially brought in as a replacement for Jack Edwards. In contrast, team captain Pat Cummins has been cleared to rejoin the squad after undergoing scans on his back in Sydney.

health.title: New COVID variant ‘Cicada’ spreading in US: Is it a new threat? Check symptoms and state-wise details
health.summary: A new COVID-19 variant named “Cicada” (BA.3.2) is spreading in over half of U.S. states, according to health officials. It possesses immune-evading mutations but is not currently linked to more severe illness than previous variants. Experts are monitoring its spread and potential impact, emphasizing the importance of continued vigilance in public health measures.

health.title: Rapid weight loss trend sounds alarm over 'hidden muscle loss' risk
health.summary: Orthopaedic specialists warn that popular GLP-1 therapies for rapid weight loss may lead to hidden muscle loss, potentially affecting joint health, strength, and mobility. As India’s weight-loss market grows, concerns arise over the long-term impacts of these treatments, which prioritize quick results but may overlook critical health trade-offs. Doctors emphasize the need for awareness regarding body composition changes.

health.title: Just Two Days of Oats Can Significantly Reduce Cholesterol Levels
health.summary: A study from the University of Bonn reveals that a two-day oat diet can significantly lower LDL cholesterol levels and improve metabolic health. Researchers found that participants experienced substantial reductions in cholesterol after only two days of consuming oats. This research highlights the potential benefits of incorporating oats into the diet for better cardiovascular health.

science.title: Send Grace and Rocky: Young stars like our Sun seen dimming outside Solar System
science.summary: A study using NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory data indicates that young stars similar to the Sun may become less hostile to life sooner than previously thought. This finding suggests that planets orbiting these stars could better retain their atmospheres and develop conditions suitable for life, potentially increasing the chances of discovering habitable worlds beyond our Solar System.

science.title: When is Aurora Peak Visibility in the US?
science.summary: A G2 geomagnetic storm is expected to enhance Northern Lights visibility across 20 U.S. states. Skywatchers in the northern regions are particularly encouraged to observe the phenomenon as the NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center forecasts increased geomagnetic activity. This event presents a significant opportunity for viewing the aurora borealis, attracting attention from both enthusiasts and casual observers alike.

science.title: Mars' Ancient Ocean: Unearthing the Red Planet's Wet History
science.summary: Recent findings indicate that Mars may have once harbored a vast ocean on its northern plains. This theory, supported by decades of satellite imagery and rover data, suggests that the planet's landscape was shaped by significant bodies of water, similar to Earth's oceans. The evidence continues to stimulate scientific discussions regarding Mars' wet history and geological evolution.

entertainment.title: 'She's a mother, she's has earned that place': Kangana Ranaut backs Deepika Padukone's eight-hour workday demand
entertainment.summary: Kangana Ranaut has expressed strong support for Deepika Padukone's demand for an eight-hour workday, following Padukone's exits from major projects like "Spirit" and "Kalki 2898 AD." Ranaut emphasized that as a leading actress and mother, Padukone has "earned" her request. She criticized the long hours women are often subjected to in the industry, recalling their previous 12-14 hour shifts.

entertainment.title: TRP Toppers: Smriti Irani's Kyunki 2 UNAFFECTED by IPL fever, Anupamaa & Naagin 7 suffer the BLOW
entertainment.summary: India Forums has released the latest TRP charts, revealing the top five shows for the week. Notably, "Kyunki 2" remains unaffected by the ongoing IPL season, maintaining its position. In contrast, popular shows "Anupamaa" and "Naagin 7" have experienced a decline in viewership. The updated rankings provide insights into the current performance of various television programs in India.

entertainment.title: Who is Jubin Nautiyal’s wife? Saiyaara singer reportedly gets married in private ceremony in Uttarakhand
entertainment.summary: Jubin Nautiyal is reportedly married in a private ceremony in Uttarakhand, his hometown. The event was attended by close family and friends, emphasizing the singer's preference for a low-key personal life. Reports indicate that he married his childhood sweetheart in a traditional ceremony, keeping the details of the wedding away from the public eye and media attention.
